figures.caption: Fig. 1: HyperKKLdyn training architecture. The shared GRU processes the input window u[tk−ω,tk] to produce a context embedding ht, from which separate MLP heads predict weight perturbations θ̃(tk) and η̃(tk). These are added to the frozen base weights θ and η, yielding an input-conditioned encoder and decoder. The losses Lpde and Lrec are backpropagated only through the hypernetwork parameters ψ.
TABLE I: State estimation SMAPE averaged over 100 trials. Lower is better. Bold: best per column. ↓: improvement over Autonomous.

Fig. 2: Estimation results across all benchmark systems over 100 trials with random initial conditions. (a) HyperKKLobs tracks the ground truth across input transitions, while the Autonomous baseline accumulates persistent phase drift. (b)– (e) SMAPE distributions confirm statistically robust improvements.
